article.description: — Nikola Jokic had 31 points, 20 rebounds and 12 assists, Jamal Murray scored 39 points and the Denver Nuggets rallied to beat San Antonio 136-131 on Thursday night, snapping the Spurs' five-game winning streak as Victor Wembanyama sat out with an...
article.headline: Jokic, Murray rally Nuggets from 20-point deficit to beat Spurs 136-131 in Wembanyama's absence

images.caption: Nikola Jokic scores 31 points and adds 20 rebounds in the Nuggets' win over the Spurs.
images.name: Nikola Jokic goes for 31 and 20 in Nuggets' win

article.linkText: Jokic, Murray rally Nuggets from 20-point deficit to beat Spurs 136-131 in Wembanyama's absence
article.story: SAN ANTONIO -- — Nikola Jokic had 31 points, 20 rebounds and 12 assists, Jamal Murray scored 39 points and the Denver Nuggets rallied to beat San Antonio 136-131 on Thursday night, snapping the Spurs' five-game winning streak as Victor Wembanyama sat out with an injury. San Antonio guard Stephon Castle had 30 points, 11 rebounds and 10 assists for his third career triple-double. With the Spurs trailing 132-129, Castle had a 3-pointer rattle off the rim with 20 seconds remaining. Murray sealed the victory with four straight free throws. After trailing by 20 points early in the third quarter, Murray's 3-pointer gave Denver its first lead at 121-119 with 4:38 remaining. Murray's basket was the start of an 11-0 run. Wembanyama was questionable due to right ankle soreness and ruled out after going through pregame warmups. He must play in 13 of San Antonio’s final 16 games for end-of-season award eligibility. The Spurs are 10-5 without Wembanyama, who watched from the second row of the team’s bench. San Antonio lost for the first time in 22 games when holding a 20-point lead. Denver outscored San Antonio 42-25 in the fourth quarter behind Murray, Jokic, Spencer Jones, Cam Johnson and Christian Braun. Jones finished with 19 points, Johnson had 15 and Braun added 11. De'Aaron Fox had 27 points and Devin Vassell scored 18 for San Antonio, which had won 16 of 17. Up next Nuggets: At Los Angeles Lakers on Saturday. Spurs: At Milwaukee on Tuesday night. ------ AP NBA: https://apnews.com/hub/NBA The Spurs have won five straight games and 16 of their past 17 games. San Antonio is 24-8 since the calendar turned to 2026 and undefeated during an 11-game stretch in February. The Nuggets will play the second game of a back-to-back set on the heels of a 129-93 win over visiting Houston on Wednesday. Jamal Murray led Denver with 30 points in the victory while Christian Braun had 19, Cameron Johnson added 17 and three-time NBA MVP Nikola Jokic collected 16 points, 13 assists and 12 rebounds. Denver shot 55.2% from the floor and forced 15 turnovers while outscoring the Rockets by 30 points after halftime.

images.caption: Victor Wembanyama (center left) and the San Antonio Spurs are favored entering the NBA Finals.
images.credit: Jesse D. Garrabrant/NBAE via Getty Images
images.name: Victor Wembanyama [1296x729]

images.caption: Shai Gilgeous-Alexander and Victor Wembanyama are Nos. 1 and 2 in the Finals MVP odds, respectively.

images.alt: Shai Gilgeous-Alexander
images.caption: Shai Gilgeous-Alexander and the Thunder are back to being odds-on title favorites after taking a 3-2 series lead over the Spurs.

images.alt: Jalen Brunson
images.caption: Jalen Brunson has the New York Knicks in the NBA Finals for the first time since 1999.
images.name: Jalen Brunson [608x342]

images.caption: The Knicks are looking to end the series against the Cavaliers tonight, as they have the second-shortest title odds and are an overwhelming favorite to win the East.

images.alt: Jalen Brunson
images.caption: The Knicks are now heavy favorites to win the Eastern Conference after taking a 3-0 series lead over the Cavs.
images.credit: Nathaniel S. Butler/NBAE via Getty Images
images.name: Jalen Brunson [608x342]

images.caption: Victor Wembanyama and the Spurs continue to see their championship odds shift.
images.name: Victor Wembanyama [1296x729]

images.name: Victor Wembanyama [1296x729]

images.alt: Oklahoma City Thunder guard Shai Gilgeous-Alexander (2) against the Phoenix Suns in the first half at Mortgage Matchup Center.
images.caption: Even after a rough stretch of games, the Thunder remain the heavy favorites to win the NBA championship.
images.name: Shai Gilgeous-Alexander [1296x729]

images.alt: Cleveland Cavaliers guard Donovan Mitchell (45) against the Phoenix Suns in the first half at Mortgage Matchup Center.
images.caption: Adding James Harden to a talented roster with the likes of Donovan Mitchell gives the Cavs a higher upside this season.
images.name: Donovan Mitchell [1296x729]

images.alt: Jaylen Brown #7 of the Boston Celtics shoots against the Portland Trail Blazers at TD Garden on January 26, 2026 in Boston, Massachusetts.
images.caption: The Celtics now have shorter odds to win the NBA title than the Spurs, Rockets, Knicks and Pistons.
images.name: Jaylen Brown [1296x729]

images.alt: Jamal Murray #27 of the Denver Nuggets dribbles the ball during the game against the Dallas Mavericks on January 14, 2026 at American Airlines Center in Dallas, Texas.
images.caption: Jamal Murray has led the Nuggets while Nikola Jokic recovers from injury.
